items.headline: WNBA’s new CBA sets blueprint as emerging women’s leagues chart their own path
items.description:
Mo'ne Davis was preparing for a Women's Professional Baseball League scrimmage when the WNBA and its players agreed to a landmark collective bargaining agreement last month.
Davis, who rose to stardom as a little league pitching sensation, followed developments as WNBA players negotiated historic salaries. As she enters the inaugural season of the WPBL, Davis sees those record gains as a signal to the next generation of women's leagues about what is possible.

items.headline: Syracuse and Wake Forest to meet for ACC football game in Toronto in 2027
items.description:
CHARLOTTE, N.C. (AP) — Syracuse and Wake Forest will hold their 2027 opener in Canada.
The Atlantic Coast Conference and ESPN Events announced Thursday that the Orange and Demon Deacons will meet for a Week Zero game at Toronto's BMO Field. That game would take place either Aug. 28 or 29 and would air nationally on ABC or ESPN.

items.headline: USA Basketball names Tibbetts, Nakase and White as assistants for the Women’s World Cup
items.description:
Nate Tibbetts, Natalie Nakase and Stephanie White will assist Kara Lawson coaching the U.S. women's basketball team at the FIBA Women's World Cup in September.
The trio of WNBA coaches were on staff for the World Cup qualifying tournament in March.

items.headline: A Knicks takeover in Philadelphia? Embiid’s plea to 76ers fans meets the resale market for Game 3
items.description:
PHILADELPHIA (AP) — After watching Knicks fans take over the 76ers' arena for years, Joel Embiid openly pleaded with fans not to sell their tickets on the open market for home playoff games.
“Don’t sell your tickets," Embiid said. “This is bigger than you. We need you guys.”
